channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: NFL is stenciling 'Choose Love' in an end zone for the Super Bowl to uplift country after tragedies
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The NFL is stenciling “Choose Love” in the back of one of the end zones at the Superdome for the Super Bowl on Sunday in an effort to encourage the country after a series of tragedies over the first six weeks of the year.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GM Howie Roseman's success in offseason or 'Howie season' has the Eagles in the Super Bowl again
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Howie Roseman’s journey to becoming one of the top executives in professional sports began as a unpaid summer intern with the Philadelphia Eagles in 2000.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump will attend the Super Bowl in New Orleans. He'll be the first sitting president to go
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump is going to the Super Bowl in New Orleans on Sunday, the first sitting president to do so. The U.S. Secret Service, which is responsible for protecting the president, said in a statement Tuesday that it has had staff on the ground for days preparing for his visit.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Simona Halep announces her retirement at age 33 after two Grand Slam titles and a return from a ban
item.description: Two-time Grand Slam champion Simona Halep announced her retirement from tennis at age 33 after a first-round loss at a tournament in her home country of Romania on Tuesday, ending an injury-filled comeback from a doping suspension.
item.dc:creator: HOWARD FENDRICH, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Brittney Griner could face 1-year window to take advantage of new talent on Atlanta Dream roster
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — New Atlanta Dream star Brittney Griner says her one-year deal was based on more than just positioning herself for a bigger deal when the WNBA's new collective bargaining agreement is expected to take effect in one year.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES ODUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mavs get Caleb Martin in trade that sends Quentin Grimes to Sixers, AP sources say
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — The Dallas Mavericks are acquiring Caleb Martin in a trade with Philadelphia that sends Quentin Grimes to the 76ers, two people with knowledge of the deal said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: SCHUYLER DIXON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: NFL to remove 'End Racism' phrase from end zones for Super Bowl LIX
item.description: The NFL will not feature the words "End Racism" in the end zone of Super Bowl LIX this weekend. The move came as Roger Goodell touted the league's DEI policies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tiger Woods' mother, Kultida Woods, has died
item.description: Kultida Woods, the Thai-born mother of Tiger Woods, died Tuesday morning, a week after being seen attending his indoor TMRW Golf League in Florida.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON AP Golf Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: From cheeseburger delivery man to architect of a dynasty: Chiefs GM Brett Veach keeps KC humming
item.description: Chiefs coach Andy Reid first met Brett Veach after he had been recommended for a coaching internship while Big Red was still with the Eagles.
item.dc:creator: Dave Skretta, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Kai Cenat, Druski, Mickey Guyton and coach 2 Chainz announced for NBA All-Star Celebrity Game
item.description: Streamer Kai Cenat, comedian-creator Druski and musical artists Mickey Guyton and Shaboozey have been announced as participants in the NBA All-Star Celebrity Game, while multi-platinum rapper 2 Chainz was named a coach.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The 2025 Super Bowl commercials already catching attention
item.description: Super Bowl Sunday isn't here yet, but some of the commercials set to air during the game have already been released.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: Luka Doncic is excited to join the Lakers after the shock of his stunning trade away from Dallas
item.description: Luka Doncic says he was just as shocked as the rest of the basketball world when the Dallas Mavericks traded him to the Los Angeles Lakers.
item.dc:creator: Greg Beacham, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Cooper Kupp says the Rams intend to trade him. He's disappointed to end his 8 years with the team
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Receiver Cooper Kupp says the Los Angeles Rams are attempting to trade him after eight seasons with the club, and he doesn't like the decision.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Eagles' Vic Fangio recalls fearing the worst during Super Bowl blackout in 2013
item.description: Philadelphia Eagles defensive coordinator Vic Fangio admitted he feared the worst when he the lights went out at the Superdome in Super Bowl XLVII.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Travis Kelce in no hurry to retire after Super Bowl
item.description: Kelce did say his relationship with Swift helps him on the football field by providing motivation that he needs to work as hard as she does.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chiefs' Butker enters his 5th Super Bowl confident in his kicking and his beliefs
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Kansas City Chiefs kicker Harrison Butker, who tends to be talkative on the subject of his Christian faith, briefly went silent on Monday night when he was asked, “What do you think about gays?” “I understand that this is a great evening and we’re here to focus on the game,” Butke
item.dc:creator: BRETT MARTEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: No. 16 Kansas shuts down No. 8 Iowa State in desperation game for both teams in the Big 12 race
item.description: LAWRENCE, Kan. (AP) — Kansas had just turned the ball over in the first half against Iowa State on Monday night when it suddenly popped loose again along the sideline, and Jayhawks guard Dajuan Harris Jr. made perhaps the hustle play of the game.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lions agree to hire Broncos' John Morton as OC and promote Kelvin Sheppard to DC, AP source says
item.description: The Detroit Lions have agreed to hire Denver Broncos assistant John Morton as offensive coordinator and promote Kelvin Sheppard to defensive coordinator, according to a person familiar with the situation.
item.dc:creator: LARRY LAGE, Associated Press, Fox 5
